title: Transactional Outbox, Relay, Inbox, Sagas, and Compensation · Compiler Case: Saga Compensation
summary: An HTTP transaction commits database state then publishes, or publishes then rolls back; a relay deletes outbox rows before confirms; a multi-service workflow treats retries as rollback. Planned artifact: Add and verify the saga compensation increment in outbox, relay, inbox, and compensation protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design saga state, commands, events, timeouts, idempotent steps, compensations, irreversibility, operator intervention, and audit evidence.
Combine consumer inbox deduplication, local domain effect, and outgoing outbox writes in one transaction to propagate effectively-once effects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Saga Compensation · Theory Saga Compensation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 6992 in a payment-effect relay after an ambiguous provider timeout. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to close the local dual-write gap and preserve confirm or external-effect ambiguity; preserve one tenant fairness invariant; then lower the byte or in-flight budget and preserve upstream rejection evidence. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
